subject: Acid bath yields stem cells: news and choice articles from F1000
body:
To ensure you receive emails from Faculty of 1000, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist.

-----------------------------------------------------

Faculty of 1000 |  http://f1000.com                
Monthly Update  |  March 2014 

-----------------------------------------------------
 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
F1000Prime in top 10% of online library resources   
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
 
An independent review in the Charleston Advisor, a leading journal for information professionals, has ranked F1000Prime in the top 10% of more than 800 online library resources. 

F1000Prime scored 4.5 out of a possible maximum of 5 stars and was most highly praised for the quality of the content in the database. Read [ http://blog.f1000.com/2014/02/07/f1000prime-in-top-10-of-online-library-resources ] more about the review on the F1000 blog. Recommend F1000Prime to your librarian here [ http://f1000.com/prime/recommend/academic ]
 

   F1000Prime                             
==================

1,017 recommendations  [ http://bit.ly/LP64sl ] added in the past 30 days*. Below is a selection:  

Regenerative acid bath   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718255373?key=izqWAChM610kp3n ]   
This remarkable study shows that exposure to simple transient stress, such as low pH, can reprogram somatic cells into pluripotent stem cells.
Obokata H, Wakayama T, Sasai Y et al. 
Nature. 2014; 505:641-7  

How to pick a graduate advisor
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718149146?key=k41vbynVinDdvkB ]
This article provides a useful perspective for students in any scientific discipline on choosing a PhD advisor.
Barres BA
Neuron. 2013; 80:275-9

Low Vit D a predictor of MS
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718246668?key=xRy3oLNFYmcEcwN ]
Another solid piece of evidence to the hypothesis that vitamin D deficiency may play a role in driving the evolution of multiple sclerosis in the very early stages of the disease.
Ascherio A, Munger KL, White R et al.
JAMA Neurol. 2014 Jan 20 [Epub ahead of print]  

*Accurate at the time of writing.

 F1000Prime Reports 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 

Open-access review journal [ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/ ], reporting on the hottest topics.    

Quality control in mitochondria: use it, break it, fix it, trash it
[ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/b/6/15 ]
It is becoming increasingly apparent that mitochondrial quality control is a factor in both aging and some neurodegenerative diseases.
F1000Prime Report by Benoît Kornmann. 
F1000Prime Rep 2014, 6:15

 ---------------
 See all recent F1000Prime Reports          
 >> http://f1000.com/prime/reports/                 
 ---------------   

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Win a £50 Amazon voucher!    
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
We're gathering feedback about F1000Research from our readers, authors and referees. 
In return, you'll be entered in a draw to win a £50 Amazon gift certificate. 
Please take our survey [ http://blog.f1000research.com/2014/02/11/survey-for-readers-authors-and-referees ] before March 11, 2014.
 
    

   F1000Research                      
======================

Our innovative open access journal [ http://f1000research.com/ ] offering rapid publication, open peer review and open data.

Below is a selection of our latest peer reviewed articles:  

DATA ARTICLE
Matched molecular pair-based data sets for computer-aided medicinal chemistry [v2; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/309]
Latest Published: 21 Feb 2014
Hu Y, de la Vega de Léon A, Zhang B et al.
F1000Research 3:36

RESEARCH ARTICLE
Genetic and environmental determinants of insect herbivore community structure in a Betula pendula population [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/2pd]
Published: 31 Jan 2014
Silfver T, Rousi M, Oksanen E et al.
F1000Research 3:34

WEB TOOL
StatsDB: platform-agnostic storage and understanding of next generation sequencing run metrics [v2; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/2x1]
Latest Published: 19 Feb 2014
Ramirez-Gonzalez RH, Leggett RM, Waite D et al.
F1000Research 2:248
  

~~~~~~~~~
Science Communication Articles    
~~~~~~~~~

F1000Research now accepts articles about science communication. This includes research or opinion articles on topics such as science education, outreach, policy, data sharing, or publishing. Throughout 2014, we are waiving the article processing charge [ http://f1000research.com/sci-comm2014 ] on these papers.

~~~~~~~~~
Article Collections  
~~~~~~~~~

F1000Research has just launched its new article collections feature. To find out more, click here [ http://blog.f1000research.com/2014/02/13/f1000research-article-collections ]


 ---------------------------------------------
  Submit now to F1000Research          
  >> http://f1000research.com/author/submit        
 ----------------------------------------------
 

    
    F1000Posters                 
======================
 
Open Repository [ http://f1000.com/posters ] for posters and slide presentations; completely free to view and submit. Below are a few selections:

* ECOLOGY *

Explosive breeding in frogs: linking phenology and priority effects
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1095005 ]
Orizaola G, Kolter N, Murillo A et al.
F1000Posters 2014,
5:82  


* PSYCHIATRY *
Brain structure and cognitive development in young children at high-risk for schizophrenia
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1095013 ]
Short SJ, Goldman BD, Woolson S et al.
F1000Posters 2013,
5:84

 ----------------------------------------------
  Deposit now        
  >> http://f1000.com/posters/fordepositors      
 ---------------------------------------------- 


  
----------------------

 * F1000Tips *

Follow @F1000Tips on Twitter [ http://twitter.com/f1000tips ] for tips, tricks, and customer support for all Faculty of 1000 service.

---------------------- 

-----------------------------------------------------   

In addition to this Monthly Update, we also send out emails featuring deals and discounts for registered users. To sign up, registered users should visit MyF1000 via any F1000 product and opt-in to receive "Information from F1000 about special offers" (under Email Alerts).

You have received this message because you asked to receive the F1000 Monthly Update to your registered email address from Faculty of 1000. If you do not wish to receive any further such messages, you may unsubscribe [ http://www.f1000.com/unsubscribe/email?email=jeeproject@yahoo.com&emailAlertType=MONTHLY_UPDATES ] at any time. For further information or enquiries please contact Customer Services at info@f1000.com [ mailto:info@f1000.com ].

Faculty of 1000 Ltd | Science Navigation Group  
Registered in England and Wales with Company Number 3739756    
Middlesex House, 34-42 Cleveland Street | London, W1T 4LB | United Kingdom
